cases.prompt:
Patient: 45 F
Indication: Aspergilloma
Airway: Tracheostomy
Procedure: Injection of Voriconazole (50mg)
Target: Left Lower Lobe (LLL)
Findings: thick tan secretions
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with Aspergilloma. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to the procedure were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

Aspergilloma

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

Aspergilloma

PROCEDURE

See procedure details below

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt: Prepare an operative report for a 96 year old female undergoing bronchoscopy for airway obstruction. Note a complication: EBUS caused a tear in the distal tracheal posterior membrane. GI consult ruled out esophageal injury. Initial studded silicone stent failed because migration occurred immediately upon deployment requiring removal. Successful placement of straight silicone stent measuring 18mm x 50mm and dilation to 18 mm.

cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with large thyroid mass / ~10 days intubation. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

RML pulmonary nodule, 40 mm

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

RML pulmonary nodule, 40 mm

PROCEDURE

Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) RML
Rigid Bronchoscopy (Therapeutic)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

BAL: Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed in the RML with saline instilled and returned. Specimens were submitted.

After induction of general anesthesia, a rigid bronchoscope was introduced via the mouth under direct visualization. A complete airway survey was performed.
Therapeutic interventions performed: Therapeutic intervention.
Hemostasis was confirmed and all instruments were withdrawn. The patient tolerated the procedure well without immediate complications.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt:
PATIENT: 58/F
INDICATION: post-transplant anastomotic stricture
PROCEDURE: Rigid Bronchoscopy, Stent Exchange (Left), Balloon Dilation (Right), BAL.
FINDINGS: Web-like stenosis at the right bronchial anastomosis; Stent in place at LMSB with thick mucus throughout.
DETAILS: Stent Ultraflex 12 x 40mm; Balloon Elation.
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with post-transplant anastomotic stricture. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

post-transplant anastomotic stricture

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

post-transplant anastomotic stricture

PROCEDURE

Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL)
Rigid Bronchoscopy (Therapeutic)
Airway Stent Placement (Ultraflex SEMS)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

BAL: Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed with saline instilled and returned. Specimens were submitted.

After induction of general anesthesia, a rigid bronchoscope was introduced via the mouth under direct visualization. A complete airway survey was performed.
Therapeutic interventions performed: Mechanical Dilation (Rigid), Airway Stent Placement.
Hemostasis was confirmed and all instruments were withdrawn. The patient tolerated the procedure well without immediate complications.

Airway dilation was performed using Balloon dilation.
Dilation sizes: 40 mm.

Airway Stent Placement The airway was measured to determine appropriate stent sizing. A stent was deployed across the target stenosis under bronchoscopic guidance.
Stent Type: Ultraflex SEMS.
Dimensions: 12mm x 40mm.

A suspected foreign body was visualized at the target airway. Retrieval was performed using Forceps.
The object was removed. Post-removal inspection showed no significant mucosal trauma.
Ventilation and airway patency were confirmed. The patient tolerated the procedure well.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt: Please generate a rigid bronchoscopy report. Patient is a 58 year old female with recurrent granulation tissue. We found a 80% obstruction in the Left Lower Lobe. Used APC and mechanical debulking to open it. Placed a Bonastent 10x20 stent in the LLL basal subsegment. Also did a BAL of the Left Upper Lobe.

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with airway obstruction requiring intervention. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

airway obstruction requiring intervention

airway obstruction requiring intervention

Rigid Bronchoscopy (Therapeutic)
Endobronchial Tumor Destruction (APC)
Airway Stent Placement (Bonastent)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

After induction of general anesthesia, a rigid bronchoscope was introduced via the mouth under direct visualization. A complete airway survey was performed.
Therapeutic interventions performed: Argon Plasma Coagulation (APC), Airway Stent Placement.
Hemostasis was confirmed and all instruments were withdrawn. The patient tolerated the procedure well without immediate complications.

Argon Plasma Coagulation (APC): Endobronchial obstruction was treated with Argon Plasma Coagulation (APC) for tumor ablation and hemostasis. Performed at the LLL.

Airway Stent Placement The airway was measured to determine appropriate stent sizing. A stent was deployed across the target stenosis under bronchoscopic guidance.
Stent Type: Bonastent.
Dimensions: 10mm x 20mm.
Location: LLL.

cases.prompt: Please generate an IP op note for a 59 year old female with mediastinal adenopathy and pulmonary nodules. We did a BAL at the Lateral Segment of RML and EBBX at multiple carinas. Used radial EBUS for a LLL lesion, taking biopsies. Staged mediastinum, sampled Station 7 which was Type 3, skipped the smaller nodes.
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with a LLL lesion, taking. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

a LLL lesion, taking

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

a LLL lesion, taking

PROCEDURE

Radial EBUS localization
Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) RML
Endobronchial Ultrasound-Guided Transbronchial Needle Aspiration (EBUS-TBNA) (Stations 7)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Localization: Radial EBUS was performed.

RML Target Sampling

BAL: Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed in the RML with saline instilled and returned. Specimens were submitted.

EBUS-TBNA Staging The endobronchial ultrasound-capable bronchoscope was introduced and a systematic mediastinal and hilar survey was performed. The following lymph node stations were identified and sampled based on ultrasound criteria:

Station 7 (Subcarinal):Biopsy Tools: TBNA.

Prior to withdrawal of the bronchoscope, inspection demonstrated no evidence of bleeding. The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt: Bronchoscopy report. 59yo M. Indication: Lung nodule. Procedure: Ion nav bronch to LUL Apicoposterior Segment (LB1+2) + EBUS. Findings: 0.8 cm Concentric nodule. Sampling: TBNA x5, TBBX x4, BAL. EBUS sampled Station 4R. Fiducial placed. No comps.
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a 59-year-old male who presents with Lung nodule. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

LUL pulmonary nodule, 8 mm

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

LUL pulmonary nodule, 8 mm

PROCEDURE

Linear Endobronchial Ultrasound (EBUS) with Transbronchial Needle Aspiration (TBNA)
Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to LUL target
Radial EBUS localization (concentric view)
Transbronchial needle aspiration of LUL target (5 passes)
Transbronchial biopsy of LUL target (4 samples)
Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL)
Fiducial marker placement

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform; TBNA needles; fiducial markers.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

A fiducial marker was loaded and placed under fluoroscopic guidance at the targeted airway location LUL.
Placement was confirmed and the bronchoscope withdrawn without complication.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — LUL Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned LUL target (8 mm).

Localization: Radial EBUS was performed.
Target Confirmation: rEBUS demonstrated concentric view of the lesion.
LUL Target Sampling

BAL: Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed with saline instilled and returned. Specimens were submitted.

Biopsy: Transbronchial forceps biopsies were performed with a total of 4 samples collected.

TBNA: Transbronchial needle aspiration was performed. A total of 5 passes were obtained from the LUL target.

EBUS-TBNA Staging The endobronchial ultrasound-capable bronchoscope was introduced and a systematic mediastinal and hilar survey was performed. The following lymph node stations were identified and sampled based on ultrasound criteria:

Station 4R (Right Lower Paratracheal):Biopsy Tools: TBNA.

Prior to withdrawal of the bronchoscope, inspection demonstrated no evidence of bleeding. The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

SPECIMENS

Station 4R EBUS aspirates — Cytology, cell block
LUL nodule TBNA (5 passes) — cytology
LUL Transbronchial biopsy (4 samples) — histology
LUL BAL — microbiology/cytology

IMPRESSION / PLAN

EBUS staging performed at stations 4R.

LUL nodule (8mm) successfully sampled via robotic bronchoscopy.

Navigated to LUL with concentric rEBUS signal.

Fiducial marker placed.

Await final pathology and cytology.

Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging to assess for PTX per local workflow.

cases.prompt: Please write a procedure note for a 72 year old female with a Parapneumonic Effusion. We performed a bedside ultrasound and subsequent fibrinolytic instillation on the left. Ultrasound showed Small volume with Thick loculations. We gave dose number 6 of tPA / DNase.
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with a 72 year old female with a Parapneumonic Effusion. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to the procedure were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

a 72 year old female with a Parapneumonic Effusion

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

a 72 year old female with a Parapneumonic Effusion

PROCEDURE

Image-Guided Chest Tube

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

After chest ultrasound documentation with ultrasound guidance, the left chest was prepped and draped in sterile fashion.
A thoracostomy tube was inserted into the unspecified intercostal space along the mid-axillary using the Seldinger technique and connected to a drainage system.
Placement was confirmed and secured. Intrapleural fibrinolytic therapy was documented using tPA + DNase.
The patient tolerated the procedure well without immediate complications.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt: 41yo F D14.31. Ion bronch RUL 1.5 cm lesion. tortuous airway anatomy so used balloon. EBUS 7 Sampled. rose conclusive for adenocarcinoma. no comps.
cases.markdown:

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a 41-year-old female who presents with adenocarcinoma. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

RUL pulmonary nodule, 15 mm

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

RUL pulmonary nodule, 15 mm

PROCEDURE

Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to RUL target

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]
INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — RUL Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned RUL target.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with Not documented. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

Not documented

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

Not documented

PROCEDURE

Linear Endobronchial Ultrasound (EBUS) with Transbronchial Needle Aspiration (TBNA)
Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to RLL target

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]

INSTRUMENTATION Ion robotic bronchoscopy platform.

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS None

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

Robotic Navigational Bronchoscopy (Ion) — RLL Nodule A CT-based navigation plan was loaded and registration was completed. The robotic catheter was advanced to the planned RLL target.

EBUS-TBNA Staging The endobronchial ultrasound-capable bronchoscope was introduced and a systematic mediastinal and hilar survey was performed. The following lymph node stations were identified and sampled based on ultrasound criteria:

Station 4L (Left Lower Paratracheal):Biopsy Tools: TBNA.

Prior to withdrawal of the bronchoscope, inspection demonstrated no evidence of bleeding. The patient tolerated the procedure well. There were no immediate complications.

SPECIMENS

Station 4L EBUS aspirates — Cytology, cell block

IMPRESSION / PLAN

EBUS staging performed at stations 4L.

RLL target successfully sampled via robotic bronchoscopy.

Await final pathology and cytology.

Post-procedure monitoring per protocol.

cases.prompt:
Patient: 71 M
Surgeon: Dr. Chen
Procedure: Bronchoscopy with Stent Removal & Cryo
Key Findings:
- LMSB stent completely occluded by inspissated secretions
- blood-tinged mucus plugs
- LUL BAL performed
Plan: Admit, polymicrobial infection, Unasyn and Dexamethasone 4 mg.

cases.prompt:
Patient: 52 M
Indication: transplant complications/stenosis
RML Procedure: Dilation, BAL, 6mm x 22mm stent.
Lingula Procedure: Attempted 7mm x 16mm stent (failed/removed), balloon dilation.
Debridement: RMS using Cryotherapy.
Complications: no active bleeding, minimal oozing
cases.markdown:
INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE REPORT

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date] CC Referred Physician: [Name]

INDICATION FOR OPERATION [Patient Name] is a [Age]-year-old [Sex] who presents with transplant complications/stenosis. The nature, purpose, risks, benefits, and alternatives to bronchoscopy were discussed with the patient in detail. The patient wished to proceed and informed consent was obtained.

CONSENT Obtained before the procedure. Indications, potential complications, and alternatives were discussed with the patient or surrogate. Consent was signed and witnessed by an assisting medical professional.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

transplant complications/stenosis

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS

transplant complications/stenosis

PROCEDURE

Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL)

ATTENDING [Name]
ASSISTANT [Name]
SUPPORT STAFF RN: [Name] RT: [Name]

ANESTHESIA Not documented

MONITORING Pulse oximetry, heart rate, telemetry, and blood pressure were continuously monitored by an independent trained observer throughout the procedure.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS Minimal

COMPLICATIONS no active bleeding, minimal oozing

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL After appropriate local anesthesia and preparation, a timeout was performed confirming patient identity, planned procedures, and laterality. Relevant procedural images were saved to the medical record.

BAL: Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed with saline instilled and returned. Specimens were submitted.

Airway dilation was performed at the RMS using Balloon dilation.
Dilation sizes: 6, 22, 7, 16 mm.

Endobronchial obstruction at RML was treated with a cryoprobe using freeze–thaw cycles.
Post-treatment patency improved and hemostasis was adequate.

A suspected foreign body was visualized at the Lingula. Retrieval was performed using Cryoprobe.
The object was removed. Post-removal inspection showed no significant mucosal trauma.
Ventilation and airway patency were confirmed. The patient tolerated the procedure well.

IMPRESSION / PLAN

Await final pathology and cytology. Post-procedure monitoring per protocol; obtain post-procedure chest imaging.

cases.prompt: Please generate an operative report for a 56 year old male referred by Dr. Banerji. Patient has bilateral transplant complications. We did a BAL, RML stenting with a 7mm x 19mm iCast fully covered, and attempted Lingula stenting which failed and required removal. We used the Erbe for RMS debridement. Note the complex dual scope work.
